definition:
1. anything projecting, a headland, promontory , (Sophocles Tragicus) 
2. anything put before one, a fence, barrier, screen , (Herdotus Historicus), attic; πρ. σώματος, of a shield, (Aeschulus Tragicus); προβλήματα ἵππων χαλκᾶ the brasen armour  of horses, (Xenophon Historicus) 
3. with genitive a defence against  a thing, πέτρων (Aeschulus Tragicus); χείματος (Euripides); κακῶν (Aristophanes Comicus) 
4. πρ. φόβου ἢ αἰδοῦς ἔχειν to have fear or shame as a defence , (Sophocles Tragicus) 
5. anything put forward as an excuse  or screen , (Demosthenes Orator); so, πρ. λαβεῖν τινά (as we say) to make a stalking horse  of him, (Sophocles Tragicus) 
6. that which is proposed, a task, business , (Euripides) 
7. a problem  in Geometry, (Plato Philosophus) (ML)
